_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C22: 85 groups across 7 registries (Reg 3 ambition, Reg 20 character, Reg 27 consciousness, Reg 58 experience, Reg 63 foolishness, Reg 129 recognition, Reg 206 vulnerability). 85 dimension updates, 4 Session B findings, 4 Session D pointers, 7 registry stamps, 1 cluster stamp. Total operations: 101

set.notes: Hospitality as leadership character qualification — stable inner orientation of welcome. Previously UNCLASSIFIED.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Positive. Primary content is relational orientation of willing welcome. AV note: description infers joy; verse names only absence of grumbling.
KEYWORD_STRONG Relational Disposition confirmed. Love for strangers as inner orientation expressed in conduct.
KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition confirmed. Benevolent inner orientation toward others.
KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition confirmed. Self-love as inverted relational orientation — disposition turned self-ward.
KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition confirmed. Inner orientation of kindly consideration toward others.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Self-seeking rivalry as inner disposition — heart oriented toward self-promotion. In-heart location confirmed by James 3:14.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Positive inner stimulation of motivation — provoking another to greater volitional response. Property term; characteristic served is motivational activation.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Agency / Power. Characteristic served is the child inner state of discouragement (athumeō). Emotion — Negative.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Inner aspiration toward pleasing God — will purposively directed toward divine approval. The God-ward direction is the content of the volition, not a dual-subject characteristic.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Vitality / Existence. Inner aspiration toward quality of personal life — the person purposing a specific mode of existence. Not Vitality/Existence.
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Moral and spiritual discernment — inner act of distinguishing what is good and pleasing to God. Both AVs confirm cognitive-moral act.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Process by which character is tested and proven — trial disclosing genuine inner quality. Testing discloses; does not create. Moral Character.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Person whose inner character has been proved genuine through testing — approved by God. Both AVs confirm proved inner character.
KEYWORD_STRONG Transformation confirmed. Testing-process as instrument producing inner transformation (faith tested → steadfastness produced).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Volition. Proven character emerging from testing — demonstrable inner quality revealed through trial. Both AVs confirm. Not Volition.
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Vitality / Existence. Quality of faith as genuinely operative — actively working, producing inner fruit. Agency/Power.

Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Vitality / Existence. Word of God as actively operative in the inner being — penetrating and discerning (Heb 4:12). Agency/Power. Dominant subject GOD. One of the most comprehensive inner-being texts in the NT.
KEYWORD_WEAK Agency / Power confirmed. Divinely produced inner-operative activities — expressions of God's power active through persons.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Theophanic appearing of God — divine self-disclosure constituting the encounter. Theological/Divine-Human. AV note: Gen 18:1 alone is a thin anchor; inner-being impact confirmed by Gen 12:7 and wider Mamre narrative.

KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed with revised dominant subject GOD. Divine seeing of inner moral life — God's perception penetrating to thoughts, intentions, heart. Gen 6:5 grounds accountability; Gen 7:1 grounds compassion.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Positive. Anchor evidence: Gen 3:6 (desire leading to Fall) and Job 32:5 (burning anger). Emotion — Negative on anchor basis. Note: group description covers full range of activations; dimension assigned on anchor verse evidence.

KEYWORD_STRONG Cognition confirmed. Spiritual blindness — seeing without inner perception; divinely-imposed failure of inner comprehension. Both AVs (Isa 6:9-10) confirm comprehensive faculty blindness.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative. Moral discipline of deliberate not-seeing — act of holiness, honour, or inner purity. Gen 9:23 grounds honour; Isa 33:15 grounds holiness/purity. Both AVs together fully confirm description.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character. Seeing God or divine glory as transforming inner-being encounter. Job 42:5 = transformation pivot; Isa 6:1 = constitutive prophetic call-encounter. Both AVs confirm Transformation.

KEYWORD_WEAK Dependence / Creatureliness confirmed. Seeing as act of faith — looking in trust, receiving divine provision through seeing. Gen 22:14 (Yhwh-Yireh) and Num 21:8 (bronze serpent) together ground description.
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Prophetic or Spirit-enabled seeing — divinely-given perceptual capacity for what is not physically present. Both AVs confirm.

KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character confirmed. Seeing as moral witness and inner obligation — conscience activated by perception of need or failure. Isa 58:7 grounds act-obligation; Hab 1:3 grounds moral witness-anguish.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition. SIGNIFICANT anchor verse mismatch: neither AV grounds the description. Gen 1:31 = divine evaluative seeing of creation; Gen 9:22 = morally charged human seeing. Description content drawn entirely from other group verses. LOW CONFIDENCE. Session B finding DIM-58-002 raised for full verse list review.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Vitality / Existence. Divine power operative within persons — God working in inner life. Phil 2:13 confirms God works to will and to work. Agency/Power dominant subject GOD.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Vitality / Existence. Corrupting or hostile power operative in persons — sinful passion (Rom 7:5) and hostile spirit (Eph 2:2). Dominant subject UNSEEN.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition. Faith, love, prayer as operative inner-being forces. Gal 5:6 and Jam 5:16 both confirm. Agency/Power dominant subject HUMAN.
KEYWORD_STRONG Transformation confirmed. Inner transformation into folly — becoming foolish through suppression of truth (Rom 1:22).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Loss of distinctive inner character — becoming flat/flavourless (Matt 5:13: salt losing taste). Identity/Selfhood.
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Folly as observed inner-opposite of wisdom — condition the searching mind investigates.
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Fool's inner condition of lacking sense and understanding — pervasive inner blindness (Jer 5:21: without heart).
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Folly as inner cognitive condition of leaders lacking wisdom.
ROOT_INFERRED Cognition confirmed. Foolishness as failure to perceive and attend to spiritual realities.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Self-boasting folly — mode of self-presentation outside God's wisdom. Identity/Selfhood.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Fool's inner disposition of rejecting wisdom — orientation of contempt toward wisdom and instruction (Prov 1:7). Relational Disposition.
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Cognition. Fool's inner volatility — quickly venting vexation (Prov 12:16). Emotion — Negative.
KEYWORD_WEAK Vitality / Existence confirmed. Consequences of the fool's inner condition — mortality resulting from foolishness (Job 5:2).
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Cognition. Foolishness of abandoning responsibility — inner disposition of negligence and self-interest over covenantal duty. Moral Character.
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Inner epistemic verdict of foolishness — how unrenewed inner being assesses spiritual truth (1 Cor 2:14).
KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition confirmed. Folly as object of the searching inner mind — heart applied to investigate (Ecc 1:17).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Foolishness as characterological disposition from within out of the heart (Mk 7:22 context). Moral Character.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Folly as characteristic heart-condition — bound up in the heart (Prov 22:15). Moral Character.
KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character confirmed. Folly as inner root of moral failure — confessed before God as sin (Ps 69:5).
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Inner condition of unpreparedness and failure to think ahead — practical wisdom failure (Matt 25:3).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Inner-epistemic reversal — becoming a fool to become wise (1 Cor 3:18). The becoming names the transformative process.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character. Blindness to what is sacred — failure of inner discernment of sacred value (Matt 23:17).
KEYWORD_WEAK Vitality / Existence confirmed. Soul poured to death — animating inner life at point of total giving (Isa 53:12). Dominant subject GOD.
KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative confirmed. Divine uncovering bringing shame and humiliation (Isa 3:17).
KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character confirmed. Violation of intimate covering carrying moral consequence (Lev 20:18).
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Inner vulnerability expressed in prayer for refuge — person seeking God as refuge in defenselessness (Ps 141:8). Dependence/Creatureliness.

Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative. Pre-fall state of nakedness without shame — original constitutive condition of the person before God (Gen 2:25). Emotion — Negative assignment explicitly wrong: verse names absence of shame. Identity/Selfhood.

Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Emotion — Negative. Radical existential nakedness — coming and returning with nothing, utterly dependent (Job 1:21). Resolves in blessing not negative emotion. Dependence/Creatureliness.
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Emotion — Negative. Nakedness as prophetic expression — body and inner being made visible sign (Isa 20:2). Prophetic identity constituted through sign-act. Identity/Selfhood.
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Emotion — Negative. Nakedness of the vulnerable calling for inner-being response of compassion (Isa 58:7). Relational Disposition.
Correcting KEYWORD_STRONG Divine-Human Correspondence. Nakedness of divine judgment — shame and exposure of stripped confidence (Amos 2:16). Emotion — Negative.

Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Total exposure before God — person fully seen with no inner reality concealed (Heb 4:13). Condition constitutively defined by divine-human relationship. Theological/Divine-Human.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Person's inner state of spiritual nakedness — self-unawareness about own inner condition (Rev 3:17: not realizing they are naked). Identity/Selfhood.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character. Physical nakedness calling for compassionate inner response (Matt 25:36). Relational Disposition.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Nakedness as exposure and shaming in judgment (Rev 17:16). Emotion — Negative.
KEYWORD_WEAK Moral Character confirmed. Intimate dignity of the person protected by law (Lev 18:6). Moral Character.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Volition. God covering naked vulnerability of his people in covenantal love (Ezek 16:8). Relational Disposition dominant subject GOD.
KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative confirmed. Nakedness as shame and exposure in judgment (Isa 47:3).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Covering required for dignity in divine presence (Exo 20:26). Theological/Divine-Human.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Razor as instrument of divine shaming — stripping honour and dignity (Isa 7:20). Emotion — Negative.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Consecrated person's avoidance of razor marking inner dedication to God (Num 6:5). Theological/Divine-Human.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Inner disposition of deceit expressed through destructive speech (Ps 52:2: worker of deceit). Moral Character.
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Divine-Human Correspondence. Originary nakedness and bareness — utter vulnerability before God's care (Ezek 16:7). Dependence/Creatureliness.
KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative confirmed. Nakedness as shame and exposure in judgment (Mic 1:11).
Correcting KEYWORD_STRONG Divine-Human Correspondence. Nakedness exposed in divine judgment — public shame and disgrace (Nah 3:5). Emotion — Negative.
KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative confirmed. Inner experience of shame, fear, and self-concealment — paradigmatic response to moral failure (Gen 3:10).
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Emotion — Negative. Originary naked vulnerability before God acts in care (Ezek 16:7). Dependence/Creatureliness.
KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition confirmed. Nakedness of person in need calling for compassionate covering (Ezek 18:7).
KEYWORD_WEAK Emotion — Negative confirmed. Nakedness as covenant judgment and shame (Deut 28:48).
Correcting ROOT_INFERRED Emotion — Negative. Deliberate exploitation to expose another's nakedness — predatory inner intent (Hab 2:15). Moral Character.
KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition confirmed. Physical nakedness of vulnerable other calling for inner compassion (2 Chr 28:15).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Cognition. Inner training toward spiritual maturity — habituating discernment through constant practice (Heb 5:14). Transformation.
Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Inner habituation of heart toward vice — trained disposition toward greed (2 Pet 2:14). Transformation (degradative).
KEYWORD_STRONG Emotion — Negative confirmed. Spiritual nakedness as state of inner shame (Rev 3:18).
Correcting KEYWORD_WEAK Relational Disposition. Physical nakedness as that which cannot separate from God's love — inner security in trust (Rom 8:35). Dependence/Creatureliness.

Previously UNCLASSIFIED. Physical nakedness/deprivation as condition of apostolic vulnerability — apostolic identity constituted through pattern of weakness and suffering for Christ (1 Cor 4:11). Identity/Selfhood.

record.description: The dokimos root family (dokimazō / dokimos / dokimion / dokimē) encodes a coherent inner-being formation arc across four groups: dokimion (testing instrument = Transformation) → dokimazō in testing sense (character disclosed = Moral Character) → dokimē (proved character = Moral Character) → dokimos (the proved person = Moral Character); with dokimazō in its discernment sense (= Cognition) showing the same root serving both formation and ongoing moral discernment. Session B should examine whether this root family constitutes a unique formation arc in NT vocabulary — from testing instrument, through proved character, to the characterised person — and how it maps onto the programme's understanding of moral formation under trial.

Group 858-003 (ra.ah — seeing triggering inner response) captures a structural principle: the visual/perceptual act as a trigger mechanism for inner emotional and volitional activation. The group names a spectrum from desire to anger. Session B should examine whether ra.ah's role as inner-state activator is a consistent structural function across the Scripture corpus — specifically whether sight as inner trigger constitutes a distinct inner-being mechanism worth tracking in the programme vocabulary. The Gen 3:6 anchor (the Fall) and Job 32:5 (Elihu's anger) show this mechanism operating in both moral failure and righteous indignation contexts.

Group 858-010 has a SIGNIFICANT anchor verse mismatch confirmed on per-verse assessment. AV-1 (Gen 1:31 — God saw creation as very good) does not ground the description's claims about divine seeing of affliction/pride producing compassion/judgement. AV-2 (Gen 9:22 — Ham seeing Noah's nakedness) is a morally charged human seeing, not divine. The description's content appears entirely drawn from other verses in the group not represented in the anchor set. The current dimension assignment (Theological/Divine-Human, dominant subject NONE) has low confidence. Session B must: (a) review the full verse list for this group to verify whether the description is valid for the wider group; (b) assess whether Gen 1:31 and Gen 9:22 are correctly classified in this group or should be reclassified; (c) consider whether the anchor verse should be re-designated to a verse that actually grounds the description.

Genesis 3:10 (group 7414-001 — e.rom) is the foundational inner-being text for the vulnerability cluster: 'I was afraid, because I was naked, and I hid myself.' This encodes the primary inner-being sequence of moral failure: nakedness-awareness → fear → self-concealment. Session B should examine how this three-stage sequence (exposure of inner condition → negative emotional response → volitional self-concealment from God) structures the anthropology of inner vulnerability across the OT and NT. The contrast with Gen 2:25 (naked and not ashamed) establishes the pre-fall / post-fall inner-being contrast as a structural framework for the vulnerability cluster.

Reg 58 (experience) is dominated by property terms (ra.ah = to see/appear; energeō/energēma/energēs = to work/operative). The ten ra.ah groups serve eight distinct inner-being dimensions — Cognition, Emotion — Negative, Moral Character, Transformation, Dependence/Creatureliness, Theological/Divine-Human, and Agency/Power — demonstrating that a single property term can serve the full range of inner-being characteristics depending on context. The energeō family covers Agency/Power in three registers: divine, hostile, and inner-being forces. Session D should assess: (a) whether ra.ah and energeō belong in the same registry; (b) whether experience is a valid inner-being category or an English-word artefact; (c) how the variety of inner-being registers accessed by a single property term should be handled in cross-registry synthesis.

Reg 3 (ambition) contains a majority of terms from the philoxenos/philoxenia/philantropōs/filautos/filofronōs cluster (all Relational Disposition) alongside the core ambition terms (eritheia, filotimeomai = Volition; erethizō = Volition / Emotion — Negative). The registry title does not reflect the actual inner-being content, which is primarily a hospitality-love cluster. Session D should assess: (a) whether the philos-family terms constitute a distinct inner-being cluster from the ambition terms; (b) whether self-directed aspiration (filotimeomai) and other-directed love (filoxenia, filantropōs) are structurally opposite poles of a single self/other orientation axis; (c) whether eritheia (selfish rivalry) and filautos (self-love) together name the inverse of the hospitality cluster.

Reg 206 (vulnerability) contains the most comprehensive nakedness vocabulary in the programme — twelve Hebrew and Greek terms across 34 groups, distributing across seven dimensions: Emotion — Negative (12 groups), Dependence/Creatureliness (5), Relational Disposition (5), Identity/Selfhood (4), Theological/Divine-Human (4), Moral Character (4), Transformation (2), Vitality/Existence (1). The dimension distribution reveals that nakedness in Scripture is not primarily an emotional concept but a multi-dimensional inner-being condition. Key structural findings: (1) the pre-fall / post-fall contrast (Gen 2:25 vs. Gen 3:10) as a structural framework; (2) nakedness serving both divine judgment (shame-exposure) and divine love (covenantal covering); (3) the apostolic identity-through-vulnerability pattern (1 Cor 4:11). Session D should examine whether vulnerability constitutes a coherent inner-being theological category or is an English-word artefact grouping distinct Hebrew/Greek inner-being phenomena.

The dokimos root family (dokimazō / dokimos / dokimion / dokimē) encodes a coherent inner-being formation arc: dokimion (testing instrument = Transformation) → dokimazō (testing/discernment act = Moral Character / Cognition) → dokimē (proved character = Moral Character) → dokimos (the proved person = Moral Character). This is one of the most complete inner-being formation arcs in the NT vocabulary. Session D should assess whether this root family maps onto the programme's broader theme of character formation under trial (cf. C09 virtue vocabulary), and whether the proved/approved terminology constitutes a distinct inner-being semantic field of formation-through-testing.

operations.description: Registry 3 (ambition): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 20 (character): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 27 (consciousness): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 58 (experience): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 63 (foolishness): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 129 (recognition): Dimension Review complete under v1.9
Registry 206 (vulnerability): Dimension Review complete under v1.9

record.notes: C22 complete. Heterogeneous cluster — moral-dispositional triad (ambition/character/foolishness) + vulnerability and experience. 37/85 dimension assignments corrected from automated labels. Key findings: vulnerability vocabulary distributes across 7 dimensions; foolishness primarily Cognition with significant Moral Character secondary cluster; ra.ah serves 8 distinct dimensions confirming property-term breadth. Significant AV mismatch on 858-010 flagged via DIM-58-002. 4 Session B findings and 4 Session D pointers raised.

operations.description: Cluster C22 Dimension Review complete — stamp set
